title: Indoor Air Pollution Exposure From Solid Fuel Cooking In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for estimating household-level indoor PM2.5 exposure from solid fuel cooking in Sub-Saharan Africa, linking fuel-type surveys, kitchen-ventilation data, and modeled exposure at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: Indoor air pollution from solid fuel cooking causes 700,000 premature deaths annually in Sub-Saharan Africa, more than malaria. Women and children under five have the highest exposure. But exposure estimates rely on fuel-type surveys that don't capture ventilation, cooking duration, or fuel stacking. Connecting survey data to actual exposure levels at sub-national scale would enable better targeting of clean-cooking interventions.

success_metrics.target: Every exposure estimate links fuel-type, ventilation, and duration data with methodology.
No clean-cooking intervention recommendation without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status and review records.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
